Anastasios Kamoutsas (@StasiKamoutsas)

Today, Florida legislators passed House Bill 1279, a bill that allows school districts to forgo collective bargaining and give immediate pay increases to top-performing teachers assigned to persistently low-performing schools.

Thank you to @LeeSchools, Superintendent @Dhatherop, and the Lee County School Board for paving the way for this student-first policy that will be implemented by school districts across the state!

Florida SAVE Act on the way!


The Floridian (@Floridianpress)

Florida Republicans just got their state SAVE Act through the Legislature.

The bill would require proof of U.S. citizenship and photo ID for voter registration. 

@FloridaGOP

That he was convicted of providing material support to terrorism and retained his citizenship is ridiculous.  

Had he been denaturalized and deported the ODU terror attack would never have happened.


Fox News (@FoxNews)

6 — Diners, Drive-ins & DeSantis

Does Buccan Sandwich Shop have the best sandwich in Florida?

Palm Beach and West Palm Beach, FL

Video

Scalia’s opinion in Morrison is one of the greatest dissents in SCOTUS history.  

All the more impressive because Scalia cut against the virtually unquestioned post-Watergate narrative re: “independent” counsels.

Anyone who wants to learn about the constitutional structure of American government can learn a great deal about it by reading that opinion.


Anthony Joseph (@ajo96th)

In his unpublished dissent he wrote one of the greatest lines of any legal writing “The Court erects citadels in ul

The Florida version of the SAVE Act is about to pass the Legislature.  

Although Florida has already enacted much of what the federal legislation contemplates, this will further fortify our state as the leader in election integrity.


Eric Daugherty (@EricLDaugh)

🚨 BREAKING: The red state of Florida is about to pass its OWN SAVE AMERICA ACT and send it to Gov. Ron DeSantis for signature

The bill FORTIFIES citizenship verification for all voters and marks pen and paper as the default method of

You mean the same unions that delayed the teacher pay increases the state enacted into law so they could leverage other union priorities against those pay increases?

We have $1.38 billion in current year that can only be used by districts for teacher pay.  That was our initiative, not the school unions’ initiative.  

Or what about when the school unions sued the state seeking the closure of schools in June of 2020?  Would it have been better for kids had they been locked out of school for the

Huge win to hold partisan school unions accountable!


Ryan Petty (@rpetty)

Thank you Florida House for passing SB 1296 a bill which will hold teachers unions accountable. The bill passed the Florida Senate earlier. 

It now goes to @GovRonDeSantis for signature!

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/rpetty/status/2031830276171592051#m

Which is why Scalia dissented in Kelo.




Ron Bowers (@RonFromAberdeen)

Scalia was mostly great but Kelo v. New London is an awful decision



— https://nitter.net/RonFromAberdeen/status/2031837926665928832#m

It’s on the way, stay tuned.


NK (@NKreformed)

Governor, Lincoln gets enough love—what about Andrew Jackson? First Florida governor! The left has tried to demonize him but he’s truly one of the greatest Americans in history.



— https://nitter.net/NKreformed/status/2031834044313297261#m

Coolidge has been the hopper for a while.  Stay tuned.


Sean Nelson 🐊 (@BunglesLife)

And how about Calvin Coolidge? Somehow it feels like DeSantis is related. 😊



— https://nitter.net/BunglesLife/status/2031824870665785453#m

1 (Hamilton), 10 (Madison), 45 (Madison), 51 (Madison), 58 (Madison), 70 (Hamilton), 78 (Hamilton), 84 (Hamilton)


Bulkington (@BulkingtonBooks)

What are some of your personal favorite Federalist Essays? And which ones might be most relevant now?





— https://nitter.net/BulkingtonBooks/status/2031826794765037750#m

The Federalist essays were high-level political discourse.  You needed to be capable of reading at a high level but also have a baseline of historical acknowledge to understand the context and the references.


Mansplainer (@Virawasp4080)

What shames me the most, as an American, is that we no longer speak & write with such eloquence and regal disposition. Most Americans can’t even write in cursive anymore, just big, dumb block letters that convey little & speak volumes in terms of our e

Lincoln is in the hopper.  Location is still TBD.


Raylene/Satomi Kozakura (@SilencedEmoGirl)

Mr. DeSantis, would you unveil a statue of Abraham Lincoln one day? That would be one of the best statues to have in Florida. The Roosevelts deserve their own statues too (Fun Fact: I’m related to both of them)



— https://nitter.net/SilencedEmoGirl/status/2031781359891464505#m

Also I think it’s an accurate statement of consumer preference that “Florida runs on Dunkin.”


Casey DeSantis (@CaseyDeSantis)

The exodus to the Free State of Florida continues…

You’re here because Governor DeSantis’ leadership built something worth fleeing to—Please don’t bring what you fled from.





— https://nitter.net/CaseyDeSantis/status/2031719536542572565#m

Hamilton, The Federalist No. 31, writing about first principles:

“IN DISQUISITIONS of every kind, there are certain primary truths, or first principles, upon which all subsequent reasonings must depend. These contain an internal evidence which, antecedent to all reflection or combination, commands the assent of the mind. Where it produces not this effect, it must proceed either from some defect or disorder in the organs of perception, or from the influence of some strong interest, or passion, o
